current_safe_actions.current_scope: Inspect source, representation, provenance, compatibility, absence, or comparison summaries through local/static surfaces.
Preview bounded text/member summaries from committed fixture-backed data only.
Read static docs, public data summaries, snapshot seed manifests, and bounded local fixture text.
Show source/evidence labels and cite-like provenance fields already present in governed records.
Export deterministic local resolution manifests for known bounded records.
Display source, evidence, fixture, and placeholder posture without rewriting truth.
Compare explicit bounded target pairs and show disagreements without merging identity.
Show checked-source and near-match absence reports for current local/search paths.
View governed source registry records and capability/coverage summaries.

current_bounded_actions.current_scope: Fetch tiny committed local fixture payloads by explicit representation id only.
current_bounded_actions.notes: This is not a download surface and not live acquisition.

current_bounded_actions.current_scope: Preview bounded member text from committed local bundle fixtures.
current_bounded_actions.notes: Static demos may show committed text summaries, not arbitrary member extraction.

current_bounded_actions.current_scope: Write deterministic manifest JSON only under caller-selected local developer paths.
current_bounded_actions.notes: Public-alpha blocks caller-provided local output paths.

current_bounded_actions.current_scope: Store bounded manifest/bundle artifacts in a local developer store.
current_bounded_actions.notes: This is local developer storage, not public mirroring or download hosting.

action_classes.notes: Safe actions can present existing governed/static/local fixture-backed facts but must preserve uncertainty.
Current local-only actions remain bounded to deterministic fixtures, manifests, and developer-selected local paths.
No public download surface or mirroring behavior exists.
Install handoff remains future and user-initiated only; install automation is not implemented.
No private upload, telemetry, account, or feedback surface exists.

required_user_confirmations.confirmation: Confirm local write path and that output is a local developer artifact, not a public download.
Confirm source, rights/access label, hash availability, executable-risk warning, and operator policy before any future download or mirror action.
Confirm user-initiated handoff, source/provenance, hash/signature display, compatibility caveats, executable risk, and rollback limitations before any future install or execute handoff.

public_alpha_defaults.notes: Public-alpha remains read-only/metadata-first and blocks risky action surfaces.

static_site_defaults.notes: GitHub Pages/static publication may show policy and metadata but must not offer download, install, execute, or mirror behavior.

native_client_requirements: Native clients must display action class and risk warnings before future handoff actions.
Native clients must show source, provenance, compatibility caveats, rights/access label or unknown state, and hash/checksum availability before future handoff.
Native clients must distinguish install handoff from install automation.
Native clients must not claim malware safety, executable trust, or rights clearance unless future evidence and policy explicitly support that claim.
Native clients must keep download, mirror, execute, installer, package-manager, restore, uninstall, and rollback actions disabled until future policy and implementation exist.

local_cache_privacy_policy_dependency.notes: Native Local Cache / Privacy Policy v0 governs future private uploads, diagnostics, cache roots, user state, local path redaction, and telemetry posture.

snapshot_consumer_requirements: Snapshot consumers may inspect, preview, read, and verify checksums.
Snapshot consumers must not treat snapshot presence as permission to download, install, mirror, execute, or restore software.
Snapshot consumers must state that v0 signatures are placeholders and that checksums do not prove executable safety.
relay_requirements: Relay projections must remain public/read-only by default.
Relay surfaces must not expose download, install, execute, mirror, private upload, write/admin, or live-probe actions to old/insecure clients.
Relay surfaces must preserve rights/access and executable-risk warnings if future policy allows any handoff metadata.

rights_policy_dependency.notes: Source metadata is not rights clearance. Future downloads, mirrors, and artifact distribution require rights/access review.

security_policy_dependency.notes: No scanner, sandbox, quarantine, or executable-safety claim exists in v0.

notes: Native Action / Download / Install Policy v0 is policy, contract, validation, and documentation only.
No download surface, installer, package-manager integration, native client, relay runtime, live probe, malware scanner, rights-clearance workflow, or executable trust claim is implemented.
Current bounded local actions remain fixture/manifest/developer-local only and do not imply public downloads or install automation.
Future native clients must use this policy before adding download, install handoff, package-manager handoff, mirror, execute, restore, uninstall, or rollback behavior.
